title: TeoNAM STAM — MLM (Q+K) GWAS on the authentic 118K panel (Fig 4C)
text:
Notes

Canonical, tracked source for the 118K MLM: scripts/teonam_mlm118k.R (build → K → MLM). Outputs: data/teonam/tassel/mlm_118k2.txt, results/sim/teonam/mlm_118k_timings.csv.
No densification. The 118K GWAS panel is ~2.7% missing, so the MLM runs on it directly (TASSEL handles per-site missing) — the Manhattan keeps its natural scatter. This is the faithful Fig-4C model that the interpolated 51K MLM notebook explicitly could not provide (it densified the 73%-missing map panel → plateau + over-correction).
Model still OLS-vs-MLM only. The nested (family:marker) specification — Chen’s primary JLM — and the additive-only (1-df) vs joint (2-df) choice are the same open model-specification points discussed in the interpolated notebook; a LOCO over-correction diagnostic is not needed here (no interpolation pseudo-replication), and is left as optional future work.


:::

title: TeoNAM STAM — MLM (Q+K) GWAS on the interpolated map panel
text:
What this is. The GWAS model Chen 2019 actually used for Fig 4C — a linear mixed model with population structure (Q) and kinship (K) — run in TASSEL5 (native arm64), and contrasted with the fixed-effect OLS scan (teonam-stam-ols-gwas-family-imputed). A mixed model needs a complete genotype matrix, so this runs on the step-interpolated complete matrix (teonam_map_v5_gwas, 51,004 markers × 1,237 lines), not the sparse observed set. This is the full GWAS marker set — the same 51,004 markers as the family-imputed OLS scan (cross-family gaps densified rather than left NA) — so the OLS↔︎MLM comparison is like-for-like on one marker set. The ~3,300 markers that share a consensus-map cM interpolate to identical genotype rows (and identical p-values); they keep their real bp, so they appear as short terraces on the Manhattan rather than being dropped. Compute lives in scripts/teonam_mlm_interp.R; this notebook loads, documents, and plots.
library(here)
library(data.table)
library(ggplot2)
theme_set(theme_bw(base_size = 13))
LOD <- 5   # Chen 2019 significance line: P < 1e-5

# genomic inflation factor: median(chi^2_obs) / median(chi^2_null)
lambda_gc <- function(p) {
  p <- p[is.finite(p) & p > 0 & p <= 1]
  round(median(qchisq(p, df = 1, lower.tail = FALSE)) / qchisq(0.5, df = 1), 3)
}

# Manhattan in the teonam-stam-ols-gwas-family-imputed style (odd chr grey60 / even black,
# LOD dotted, chromosome axis). Input: data.table(CHR, BP, logP).
manhattan <- function(d, title) {
  d <- d[order(CHR, BP)]
  off <- d[, .(mx = max(BP)), by = CHR][order(CHR)]
  off[, base := cumsum(as.numeric(shift(mx, fill = 0)))]
  d <- merge(d, off[, .(CHR, base)], by = "CHR")
  d[, x := as.numeric(BP) + base]
  axis <- d[, .(mid = (min(x) + max(x)) / 2), by = CHR][order(CHR)]
  ggplot(d, aes(x, logP, colour = factor(CHR %% 2))) +
    geom_point(size = 0.5) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = LOD, linetype = "dotted") +
    scale_colour_manual(values = c("grey60", "black"), guide = "none") +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = axis$mid, labels = axis$CHR, expand = c(0.01, 0)) +
    labs(x = "chromosome", y = expression(-log[10](italic(P))), title = title)
}

title: TeoNAM STAM — MLM (Q+K) GWAS on the interpolated map panel
text:
The model
The previous notebook fit OLS (fixed effects only):
\[
y_i \;=\; \mu \;+\; \sum_f \beta_f\,\mathbb{1}[\text{family}_i=f] \;+\; \gamma\,g_{im} \;+\; \varepsilon_i .
\]
Chen’s GWAS fits a linear mixed model (Yu et al. 2006), adding a polygenic random term with covariance given by the kinship matrix:
\[
\mathbf{y} \;=\; \mathbf{1}\mu \;+\; \mathbf{Q}\boldsymbol{\alpha}
\;+\; \mathbf{g}_a\,\gamma_a \;+\; \mathbf{g}_d\,\gamma_d
\;+\; \mathbf{Z}\mathbf{u} \;+\; \boldsymbol{\varepsilon},
\qquad
\mathbf{u}\sim N(\mathbf 0,\,\mathbf K\sigma^2_g),\;\;
\boldsymbol{\varepsilon}\sim N(\mathbf 0,\,\mathbf I\sigma^2_e).
\]

\(\mathbf{Q}\) — first 5 principal components (fixed, population structure).
\(\mathbf{g}_a\) — additive marker dosage (0/1/2), effect \(\gamma_a\); \(\mathbf{g}_d\) — dominance indicator (1 if heterozygous), effect \(\gamma_d\). Both are TASSEL’s default; the marker is tested by the joint 2-df hypothesis \(H_0:\gamma_a=\gamma_d=0\).
\(\mathbf{u}\) — polygenic background; \(\mathbf{K}\) = centered IBS kinship.

Estimation (TASSEL, P3D). Variance components \((\sigma^2_g,\sigma^2_e)\) are fit by REML via the EMMA spectral trick (eigendecompose \(\mathbf K\) once), estimated once under the null and reused for every marker (P3D). Each marker is then a generalized-least-squares \(F\)-test (2 df: \(\gamma_a,\gamma_d\)) using \(\mathbf V^{-1}\), \(\mathbf V=\mathbf K\sigma^2_g+\mathbf I\sigma^2_e\). (The additive-only 1-df test, TASSEL’s add_p, is the alternative — arguably better for a ~homozygous RIL panel; it gives \(\lambda_{GC}\approx0.97\) vs 1.37 for the joint test.)
Why it matters. OLS omits \(\mathbf{Z}\mathbf{u}\) — no polygenic shrinkage — so test statistics inflate under structure/relatedness. The MLM’s \(\mathbf K\) term is exactly the correction; the effect is quantified below as the drop in \(\lambda_{GC}\).

title: TeoNAM STAM — MLM (Q+K) GWAS on the interpolated map panel
text:
Pipeline (scripts/teonam_mlm_interp.R, native arm64 TASSEL5)

# 1. densify the full 51,004 GWAS set per chr by step-interpolation (nilHMM) -> complete
#    51,004 x 1,237 matrix (no missing) -> hapmap geno_gwas_interp.hmp.txt
# 2. K: TASSEL Centered_IBS on the complete matrix
run_pipeline.pl -importGuess geno_gwas_interp.hmp.txt \
  -KinshipPlugin -method Centered_IBS -endPlugin -export kinship_cIBS_interp.txt -exportType SqrMatrix
# 3. Q: 5 PCs via prcomp(complete matrix) -> phenotype covariates (STAM + PC1..5)
# 4. MLM (P3D): genotype + phenotype(+Q) + K, two-stage combine
run_pipeline.pl -fork1 -h geno_gwas_interp.hmp.txt -fork2 -r pheno_stam_pc_interp.txt \
  -fork3 -k kinship_cIBS_interp.txt -combine4 -input1 -input2 -intersect \
  -combine5 -input4 -input3 -mlm -mlmVarCompEst P3D -mlmCompressionLevel None -export mlm_interp


tim <- fread(here("results/sim/teonam/mlm_interp_timings.csv"))
knitr::kable(tim, caption = "TASSEL5 step timings (native arm64, Temurin 17; MLM uses pure-Java EJML, no native BLAS)")

title: TeoNAM STAM — MLM (Q+K) GWAS on the interpolated map panel
text:
Load results

ols <- fread(here("data/teonam/stam_gwas_scan_interpolated.csv"))            # OLS on the interpolated 51,004
ols <- ols[is.finite(P) & P > 0, .(CHR = as.integer(CHR), BP = as.integer(BP), P)]
mlm <- fread(here("data/teonam/tassel/mlm_interp2.txt"))        # TASSEL MLM (for the gene table)
mlm <- mlm[Marker != "None", .(CHR = as.integer(Chr), BP = as.integer(Pos), P = as.numeric(p))][is.finite(P) & P > 0]
lc  <- fread(here("results/sim/teonam/mlm_loco_scan.csv"))      # p_full = MLM Q+K (faithful, = TASSEL), p_loco = LOCO

# the three models on the same interpolated 51,004-marker matrix
three <- rbindlist(list(
  ols[, .(CHR, BP, P, method = "OLS (no K)")],
  lc[is.finite(p_full) & p_full > 0, .(CHR = chr, BP = bp, P = p_full, method = "MLM Q+K")],
  lc[is.finite(p_loco) & p_loco > 0, .(CHR = chr, BP = bp, P = p_loco, method = "LOCO")]
))
lev  <- c("OLS (no K)", "MLM Q+K", "LOCO")
labs <- setNames(vapply(lev, function(m) sprintf("%s, λ=%.2f", m, lambda_gc(three[method == m, P])), ""), lev)
three[, mlab := factor(labs[method], levels = labs[lev])]
pal  <- setNames(c("firebrick", "steelblue4", "darkorange"), labs[lev])

title: TeoNAM STAM — MLM (Q+K) GWAS on the interpolated map panel
text:
Genomic inflation: OLS → MLM → LOCO

knitr::kable(
  three[, .(markers = .N, lambda_GC = lambda_gc(P), max_logP = round(max(-log10(P)), 2)), by = method],
  caption = "Three models on the same 51,004 interpolated markers (only the structure correction differs)."
)


Three models on the same 51,004 interpolated markers (only the structure correction differs).

The Q + K correction pulls \(\lambda_{GC}\) from 2.55 (OLS) → 1.37 (MLM) and the global peak from 17.3 → 8.7. LOCO (leave-one-chromosome-out kinship — the diagnostic detailed below) trades global control for local power, so its \(\lambda_{GC}\) runs high (≈3.4) while it revives the true peaks. Residual \(\lambda>1\) under the MLM is interpolation ties (pseudo-replication) plus structure not fully captured by 5 PCs.

title: TeoNAM STAM — MLM (Q+K) GWAS on the interpolated map panel
text:
QQ — OLS · MLM (Q+K) · LOCO (one panel)

qd <- three[, {
  p <- P[is.finite(P) & P > 0 & P <= 1]; o <- -log10(sort(p)); e <- -log10(ppoints(length(p)))
  k <- c(which(o > 2), sample(which(o <= 2), min(sum(o <= 2), 6000)))
  .(exp = e[k], obs = o[k])
}, by = mlab]
ggplot(qd, aes(exp, obs, colour = mlab)) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, colour = "grey40", linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_point(size = 0.5, alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = pal, name = NULL) +
  guides(colour = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 2.5, alpha = 1))) +
  labs(x = expression(Expected~-log[10](italic(P))), y = expression(Observed~-log[10](italic(P))),
       title = "STAM GWAS QQ: OLS vs MLM (Q+K) vs LOCO") +
  theme(legend.position = c(0.02, 0.98), legend.justification = c(0, 1),
        legend.background = element_rect(fill = scales::alpha("white", 0.7), colour = NA))








MLM (blue) hugs the null through the bulk; OLS (red) is inflated everywhere; LOCO (orange) restores the true tail but at a globally inflated baseline.

title: TeoNAM STAM — MLM (Q+K) GWAS on the interpolated map panel
section: Candidate-gene peaks: what the correction removes
text:
Candidate-gene peaks: what the correction removes
The MLM deflates the whole genome; several JLM candidate genes that clear LOD 5 under OLS drop below threshold under the mixed model — including tb1, tsh4, and zmm16. The LOCO test below confirms this is over-correction (proximal contamination — the peaks return when kinship excludes their own chromosome), not evidence against the genes.

ov <- fread(here("results/sim/teonam/stam_candidate_overlap.csv"))   # symbol, chr, start (v5)
peakfn <- function(scan, ch, ps) {
  w <- scan[CHR == ch & abs(BP - ps) <= 5e5]
  if (nrow(w)) round(max(-log10(w$P)), 2) else NA_real_
}
gt <- data.table(
  gene = ov$symbol, chr = ov$chr,
  OLS = mapply(function(c, p) peakfn(ols, c, p), ov$chr, ov$start),
  MLM = mapply(function(c, p) peakfn(mlm, c, p), ov$chr, ov$start)
)
gt[, `OLS>5` := ifelse(is.finite(OLS) & OLS >= LOD, "yes", "no")]
gt[, `MLM>5` := ifelse(is.finite(MLM) & MLM >= LOD, "yes", "no")]
knitr::kable(gt[order(-OLS)], caption = "Candidate-gene peak -log10P (±500 kb): OLS vs MLM, and LOD-5 survival.")

title: TeoNAM STAM — MLM (Q+K) GWAS on the interpolated map panel
section: LOCO test — is the loss over-correction?
text:
LOCO test — is the loss over-correction?
Is the MLM’s loss of tb1/tsh4/zmm16 genuine deflation or proximal contamination — the kinship absorbing each QTL’s own-chromosome signal? The standard diagnostic is LOCO: build the kinship from all markers except the tested marker’s chromosome. Implemented as an EMMAX in R (scripts/teonam_mlm_loco.R) that uses TASSEL’s model exactly — additive + dominance, 2-df joint F, P3D — and reproduces the full-K TASSEL MLM (λ ≈ 1.366 vs 1.365; per-gene peaks within ~0.1): a full-K pass (validation) and the LOCO pass.

lc <- fread(here("results/sim/teonam/mlm_loco_scan.csv"))     # marker, chr, bp, p_full, p_loco
peak2 <- function(pv, ch, ps) {
  w <- lc[chr == ch & abs(bp - ps) <= 5e5]; v <- -log10(w[[pv]]); v <- v[is.finite(v)]
  if (length(v)) round(max(v), 2) else NA_real_
}
loco <- data.table(
  gene = ov$symbol, chr = ov$chr,
  OLS  = gt$OLS[match(ov$symbol, gt$gene)],
  MLM  = gt$MLM[match(ov$symbol, gt$gene)],
  `EMMAX full-K` = mapply(function(c, s) peak2("p_full", c, s), ov$chr, ov$start),
  LOCO = mapply(function(c, s) peak2("p_loco", c, s), ov$chr, ov$start)
)
knitr::kable(loco[order(-OLS)], caption = sprintf(
  "Candidate-gene peak -log10P — LOCO restores the peaks (λ_GC: full-K %.2f, LOCO %.2f).",
  lambda_gc(lc$p_full), lambda_gc(lc$p_loco)))

(The full-K-vs-LOCO contrast is the middle vs bottom facet of the Manhattan above.)
Reading. The peaks return under LOCO (tb1 4.2→11.0, zmm16 4.0→10.0, tru1 5.7→12.5, an1 4.4→7.1, tsh4 2.7→5.0) — only possible if the full-K MLM was absorbing each QTL’s own-chromosome signal. So the loss is over-correction (proximal contamination), not evidence against the genes. (ts2 stays low even under LOCO — its OLS signal was genuinely mostly confounding, echoing the original ~9-vs-15 concern.)
Two notes. (1) This R-EMMAX is faithful, not an approximation: it uses TASSEL’s exact model (add+dom, 2-df joint F, P3D) and its full-K pass reproduces the TASSEL MLM (λ_GC = 1.372 vs 1.365; peaks within ~0.1). Kinship flavor is irrelevant — VanRaden and TASSEL’s Centered-IBS gave identical results. It’s also ~15× faster (R’s native Accelerate BLAS ≈30 s vs TASSEL’s pure-Java EJML ≈7 min), so it’s the engine of choice for the coverage sweep. (2) LOCO’s own genome-wide λ_GC (3.426) is inflated — it trades global control for local power on this heavily-structured interpolated panel, so LOCO here is a diagnostic, not a fix to deploy. The real fix remains chen2019_GWAS (118K directly-typed).

title: TeoNAM STAM — MLM (Q+K) GWAS on the interpolated map panel
text:
Notes

Canonical, tracked source for the MLM scan: scripts/teonam_mlm_interp.R (build → K → Q → MLM) + scripts/teonam_mlm_lambda.R (λ_GC / QQ). Outputs: data/teonam/tassel/mlm_interp2.txt, results/sim/teonam/mlm_interp_timings.csv.
Matrix: interpolated-complete (teonam_map_v5_gwas, 51,004) — required because the MLM needs a complete matrix (the sparse observed set stalls: per-marker missing defeats P3D reuse). Cost: block plateaus, no scatter.
Faithful GWAS (scatter + correct absolute peaks) needs chen2019_GWAS (118,838 directly-typed SNPs); see DATA.md and the outreach in agent/outreach_teonam_118k.md.

title: TeoNAM STAM — MLM (Q+K) GWAS on the interpolated map panel
text:
Model-specification caveats & suggestions
This MLM reproduces TASSEL faithfully, but it is not the best-specified model for this population — the QQ shows no correction reaches \(\lambda_{GC}\approx1\). Four issues, roughly in order of importance, and how to address each:

Common marker effect in a NAM (most important). The five teosinte founders (TIL01/03/11/14/25) carry different alleles, so a single additive slope on the 0/1/2 dosage pools them as if identical. The correctly-specified model nests the marker within family (family + family:marker) — which is exactly why Chen’s primary method was the JLM, with the common-effect GWAS only a complementary approximation. Fix: use the nested model; don’t treat the common-effect MLM as the target.
Additive + dominance on a ~homozygous RIL panel. The dominance term is here only to match TASSEL’s default — but with ~8% heterozygosity it is barely estimable and mostly injects noise (it is what pushes \(\lambda_{GC}\) from 0.97 to 1.37). Additive-only (1 df) is the defensible spec for RILs.
Structure term. We use \(\mathbf{Q}\) = 5 PCs (Chen’s GWAS choice); the natural cofactor for a five-family NAM is the Family factor (4 df). Note the inconsistency: the OLS scan used Family, this MLM uses PCs.
Infinitesimal-\(\mathbf{K}\) vs the actual architecture. The MLM assumes many small polygenic effects; STAM is a few large domestication QTL, and the interpolated genotypes are block-correlated (pseudo-replication). Those violations are why \(\lambda_{GC}\) stays off 1 regardless of the correction — no kinship trick fixes a fundamentally mis-modeled null.

title: TeoNAM STAM — OLS GWAS on the authentic 118K GWAS panel (Fig 4C)
text:
What this is. The computation behind the STAM OLS GWAS scan on the authentic 118,838-SNP Chen 2019 GWAS panel (Qiuyue Chen’s Drive release), lifted to B73 v5 (data/teonam/stam_gwas_scan_118k.csv), tracked in its own right. It ports the scripts/teonam_stam_gwas118k.R scan into a rendered notebook so the scan — not just its Manhattan — is reproducible from primary data. The annotated Fig-4C Manhattan that consumes this CSV lives in teonam-qtl-recovery; this notebook produces it.
This is the faithful Fig 4C marker set: Chen’s GWAS SNPs (GBS filtered at MAF > 0.01, missing rate < 0.75, then FSFHap-imputed per subpopulation, combined, and re-filtered to MAF > 0.05 / missing < 0.2 → 118,838 SNPs), lifted v2 → v5 (118,514 lifted). It replaces the 51K map-panel approximation used by teonam-stam-ols-gwas-family-imputed; the model is identical (STAM ~ Family + marker, 1-df F), so the two are directly comparable — only the marker set differs (118K GWAS panel vs 51K map panel). As there, each marker is tested only on the families that typed it (cross-family gaps left as NA, not densified), giving natural GWAS scatter.
library(here)
library(data.table)
library(parallel)
library(readxl)
library(ggplot2)
theme_set(theme_bw(base_size = 12))
THREADS <- max(1L, detectCores() - 2L)
LOD <- 5 # Chen 2019 significance line: P < 1e-5

title: TeoNAM STAM — OLS GWAS on the authentic 118K GWAS panel (Fig 4C)
text:
Inputs (primary 118K dosage + phenotype + v5 liftover)

# 118K GWAS-panel dosage: integer matrix [markers x lines], NA = untyped (0/1/2)
g118 <- readRDS(here("data/teonam/teonam_gwas118k_dosage.rds"))
dos <- g118$dos
lines <- colnames(dos)
fam <- factor(substr(lines, 1, 5)) # TIL01A001 -> TIL01

# phenotype: STAM (staminate spikelet), joined by line key
ph <- as.data.frame(read_excel(
  here("data/teonam/9250682/TeoNAM_1257RILs_22traits_phenotype_data.xlsx")
))
names(ph)[1] <- "line"
y <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(setNames(ph$STAM, ph$line)[lines]))

# v5 coordinates; restrict the scan to 118K markers that lifted to v5
mc <- fread(here("data/teonam/markers_v5_gwas118k.tsv"))
chr_by <- setNames(mc$chr_v5, mc$marker)
pos_by <- setNames(mc$pos_v5, mc$marker)
mk <- intersect(rownames(dos), mc$marker)
mk_idx <- match(mk, rownames(dos))

data.table(
  quantity = c(
    "lines", "markers (panel)", "markers (lifted, scanned)",
    "STAM phenotyped", "families"
  ),
  value = c(
    length(lines), nrow(dos), length(mk),
    sum(!is.na(y)), nlevels(fam)
  )
)

                    quantity  value
                      <char>  <int>
1:                     lines   1257
2:           markers (panel) 118838
3: markers (lifted, scanned) 118514
4:           STAM phenotyped   1256
5:                  families      5

title: TeoNAM STAM — OLS GWAS on the authentic 118K GWAS panel (Fig 4C)
text:
The model
Identical to the family-imputed 51K notebook. For each marker \(m\), restrict to the lines \(i\) with a non-missing dosage \(g_{im}\in\{0,1,2\}\) and a non-missing STAM value \(y_i\), then compare two nested least-squares fits:
\[
\textbf{Null:}\quad y_i = \mu + \sum_{f} \beta_f \,\mathbb{1}[\text{family}_i = f] + \varepsilon_i
\] \[
\textbf{Full:}\quad y_i = \mu + \sum_{f} \beta_f \,\mathbb{1}[\text{family}_i = f] + \gamma\, g_{im} + \varepsilon_i
\]
The marker enters as a single additive dosage effect \(\gamma\) shared across families (1 numerator df). Significance is the marginal \(F\)-test of \(\gamma\):
\[
F = \frac{(\mathrm{RSS}_0 - \mathrm{RSS}_1)/1}{\mathrm{RSS}_1/(n - \mathrm{rank})},
\qquad P = \Pr\big(F_{1,\,n-\mathrm{rank}} > F\big).
\]
Family is a fixed categorical covariate (levels dropped to those present for that marker). This is OLS: no kinship/polygenic random effect \(K\) — deliberately the same fixed-effects model as the 51K notebook, not Chen’s Q(5 PCs) + SNP + K(IBS) mixed model, so peak heights run hotter (the \(\lambda_{GC}\)/QQ comparison is a separate analysis).

title: TeoNAM STAM — OLS GWAS on the authentic 118K GWAS panel (Fig 4C)
TeoNAM STAM — OLS GWAS on the authentic 118K GWAS panel (Fig 4C)
text:
QC and results

fin <- scan[is.finite(P) & P > 0]
data.table(
  metric = c(
    "markers scanned", "markers tested (finite P)",
    "median lines / marker", "max -log10P",
    sprintf("genome-wide sig. (P < 1e-%d)", LOD)
  ),
  value = c(
    nrow(scan), nrow(fin), median(scan$n),
    round(max(-log10(fin$P)), 2), sum(fin$P < 10^(-LOD))
  )
)

text:
Inputs (primary 118K dosage + phenotype + v5 liftover)
# 118K GWAS-panel dosage: integer matrix [markers x lines], NA = untyped (0/1/2)
g118 <- readRDS(here("data/teonam/teonam_gwas118k_dosage.rds"))
dos <- g118$dos
lines <- colnames(dos)
fam <- factor(substr(lines, 1, 5)) # TIL01A001 -> TIL01

# phenotype: STAM (staminate spikelet), joined by line key
ph <- as.data.frame(read_excel(
  here("data/teonam/9250682/TeoNAM_1257RILs_22traits_phenotype_data.xlsx")
))
names(ph)[1] <- "line"
y <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(setNames(ph$STAM, ph$line)[lines]))

# v5 coordinates; restrict the scan to 118K markers that lifted to v5
mc <- fread(here("data/teonam/markers_v5_gwas118k.tsv"))
chr_by <- setNames(mc$chr_v5, mc$marker)
pos_by <- setNames(mc$pos_v5, mc$marker)

mk_idx <- match(mk, rownames(dos))

data.table(
  quantity = c(
    "lines", "markers (panel)", "markers (lifted, scanned)",
    "STAM phenotyped", "families"
  ),
  value = c(
    length(lines), nrow(dos), length(mk),
    sum(!is.na(y)), nlevels(fam)
  )
)

title: TeoNAM STAM — OLS GWAS on the authentic 118K GWAS panel (Fig 4C)
text:
The model
Identical to the family-imputed 51K notebook. For each marker \(m\), restrict to the lines \(i\) with a non-missing dosage \(g_{im}\in\{0,1,2\}\) and a non-missing STAM value \(y_i\), then compare two nested least-squares fits:
\[
\textbf{Null:}\quad y_i = \mu + \sum_{f} \beta_f \,\mathbb{1}[\text{family}_i = f] + \varepsilon_i
\] \[
\textbf{Full:}\quad y_i = \mu + \sum_{f} \beta_f \,\mathbb{1}[\text{family}_i = f] + \gamma\, g_{im} + \varepsilon_i
\]
The marker enters as a single additive dosage effect \(\gamma\) shared across families (1 numerator df). Significance is the marginal \(F\)-test of \(\gamma\):
\[
F = \frac{(\mathrm{RSS}_0 - \mathrm{RSS}_1)/1}{\mathrm{RSS}_1/(n - \mathrm{rank})},
\qquad P = \Pr\big(F_{1,\,n-\mathrm{rank}} > F\big).
\]
Family is a fixed categorical covariate (levels dropped to those present for that marker). This is OLS: no kinship/polygenic random effect \(K\) — deliberately the same fixed-effects model as the 51K notebook, not Chen’s Q(5 PCs) + SNP + K(IBS) mixed model, so peak heights run hotter (the \(\lambda_{GC}\)/QQ comparison is a separate analysis).

text:
Notes

This notebook is the canonical, tracked source for stam_gwas_scan_118k.csv; it ports scripts/teonam_stam_gwas118k.R verbatim (same OLS model as the 51K family-imputed notebook).
The marker set is the authentic Chen 2019 GWAS panel (118,838 SNPs → 118,514 lifted to v5), not the 51K map panel. Same model, different markers → the two OLS notebooks are directly comparable.
Model is OLS (fixed effects), no kinship term — the peak-height / \(\lambda_{GC}\) comparison against Chen’s Q + K mixed model is deferred to a dedicated inflation notebook (the 118K MLM analog is not yet built).

title: TeoNAM STAM — OLS GWAS on the family-imputed map panel (Fig 4C)
text:
What this is. The computation behind the STAM OLS GWAS scan on the family-imputed map panel (data/teonam/stam_gwas_scan_family_imputed.csv), tracked in its own right. It ports the throwaway scripts/teonam_stam_gwas_family_imputed.R into a rendered notebook so the scan — not just its Manhattan — is reproducible from primary data. The annotated Fig-4C Manhattan that consumes this CSV lives in teonam-qtl-recovery; this notebook produces it.
The genotypes are FSFHap family-imputed (teonam_map_v5_gwas, 51,004 markers — Chen’s map construction, complete within family). Each marker is tested only on the families that typed it (cross-family gaps left as NA, not densified), giving the natural GWAS scatter with no step-interpolation plateau artifact. (family_imputed names exactly this state: FSFHap within-family imputation only, without cross-family step-interpolation — contrast the interpolated matrix used by the MLM notebook.)
library(here)
library(data.table)
library(parallel)
library(readxl)
library(ggplot2)
theme_set(theme_bw(base_size = 12))
THREADS <- max(1L, detectCores() - 2L)
LOD <- 5 # Chen 2019 significance line: P < 1e-5

title: TeoNAM STAM — OLS GWAS on the family-imputed map panel (Fig 4C)
text:
Inputs (all primary data — no .rds intermediates)

# FSFHap-imputed recoded dosages (complete within family): 1237 lines x 51,482 markers; NA = cross-family untyped (0/1/2 = W22 / het / teosinte)
geno <- fread(here("data/teonam/TeoNAM_genotype_clean.csv"))

# robust key = family + line, e.g. "TIL01" + "A001"; family from the population column
key <- paste0(sub("^W22", "", geno[[2]]), sub("^.*Line_", "", geno[[1]]))
fam <- factor(sub("^W22", "", geno[[2]]))

# phenotype: STAM (staminate spikelet), joined by line key
ph <- as.data.frame(read_excel(
  here("data/teonam/9250682/TeoNAM_1257RILs_22traits_phenotype_data.xlsx")
))
names(ph)[1] <- "line"
y <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(setNames(ph$STAM, ph$line)[key]))

# v5 coordinates; restrict the scan to markers that lifted to v5
mc <- fread(here("data/teonam/markers_v5.tsv"))
chr_by <- setNames(mc$chr_v5, mc$marker)
pos_by <- setNames(mc$pos_v5, mc$marker)
mk <- intersect(names(geno)[-(1:3)], mc$marker)

data.table(
  quantity = c(
    "lines", "markers (raw)", "markers (lifted, scanned)",
    "STAM phenotyped", "families"
  ),
  value = c(
    nrow(geno), ncol(geno) - 3L, length(mk),
    sum(!is.na(y)), nlevels(fam)
  )
)

5:                  families     5

title: TeoNAM STAM — OLS GWAS on the family-imputed map panel (Fig 4C)
text:
The model
For each marker \(m\), restrict to the lines \(i\) with a non-missing dosage \(g_{im}\in\{0,1,2\}\) and a non-missing STAM value \(y_i\), then compare two nested least-squares fits:
\[
\textbf{Null:}\quad y_i = \mu + \sum_{f} \beta_f \,\mathbb{1}[\text{family}_i = f] + \varepsilon_i
\] \[
\textbf{Full:}\quad y_i = \mu + \sum_{f} \beta_f \,\mathbb{1}[\text{family}_i = f] + \gamma\, g_{im} + \varepsilon_i
\]
The marker enters as a single additive dosage effect \(\gamma\) shared across families (1 numerator df). Significance is the marginal \(F\)-test of \(\gamma\):
\[
F = \frac{(\mathrm{RSS}_0 - \mathrm{RSS}_1)/1}{\mathrm{RSS}_1/(n - \mathrm{rank})},
\qquad P = \Pr\big(F_{1,\,n-\mathrm{rank}} > F\big).
\]
Family enters as a fixed categorical covariate (levels dropped to those present for that marker) — it plays the role of the structure term. This is ordinary least squares (OLS): there is no kinship/polygenic random effect \(K\). Chen 2019 instead used a mixed model y ~ Q(5 PCs) + SNP + K(IBS); the missing random \(K\) is what makes ours “not mixed”, so peak heights here are expected to run hotter — quantifying that inflation (\(\lambda_{GC}\), QQ) is a separate analysis.

title: Inv4m tagging-SNP genotype: 50K vs 250K RTIGER calls, ZEAL NIL panel
text:
What this is. A companion to the inversion-paper ZEAL flowering notebook (scripts/inversion_paper/Zeal_Inv4m_flowering_lmm.Rmd). The genotype at the Inv4m tagging SNP PZE04175660223 (chr4:181,637,780) is read from two RTIGER ancestry call sets, both are RTIGER (HMM on skim allele counts); they differ only in the reference variant set:
Show code
library(here)
library(data.table)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggpubr)
library(scales)
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)
library(knitr)
source(here("R/plotting.R")) # state_palette(): REF=gold, HET=springgreen4, ALT=purple4

POS <- 181637780L # PZE04175660223, chr4
geno2 <- c(recurrent = "gold", donor = "purple4") # 2-state (presence)
geno3 <- state_palette() # REF / HET / ALT

title: Inv4m tagging-SNP genotype: 50K vs 250K RTIGER calls, ZEAL NIL panel
text:
Deriving the two genotype vectors


Show code
# --- 250K (previous): B73 / Introgression segment state at the locus, per line ---
L <- readRDS(here("data/zeal/rtiger_250K_calls_introfinder.rds")) # staged from BZea_Introgression_Finder
g250v <- sapply(L, function(tb) {
  tb <- as.data.frame(tb)
  s <- tb[tb$V1 == "chr4", , drop = FALSE]
  if (!nrow(s)) {
    return(NA_character_)
  }
  h <- s$V4[s$V2 <= POS & s$V3 >= POS]
  if (length(h)) h[1] else NA_character_
})
d250 <- data.table(
  NIL = sub("[.]B$", "", names(L)), # strip .B bulk suffix -> pedigree
  g250 = ifelse(is.na(g250v), NA_character_, ifelse(g250v == "B73", "recurrent", "donor"))
)
d250 <- unique(d250[!is.na(g250)], by = "NIL")

# --- 50K (current): 0/1/2 segment state at the locus, per line (via skim_id) ---
seg <- fread(here("data/zeal/rtiger_50K_calls.csv"))
ss <- fread(here("data/zeal/samplesheet_3way.csv"))
cov <- unique(seg[chr == 4L & start_bp <= POS & end_bp >= POS, .(skim_id = name, st = state)], by = "skim_id")
p2s <- unique(ss[!is.na(skim_id) & skim_id != "", .(NIL = pedigree, skim_id)], by = "NIL")
d50 <- merge(p2s, cov, by = "skim_id")[, .(NIL,
  g50_3 = factor(st, 0:2, c("REF", "HET", "ALT")),
  g50   = ifelse(st == 0L, "recurrent", "donor")
)]

# --- phenotypes + paper filters (CLY25, incl. paper's outlier plots) ---
OUTLIER_PLOTS <- c(2756, 453, 304)
cly <- fread(here("data/zeal/CLY25_ZEAL.csv"))
setnames(cly, "Genotype", "NIL")
meta <- fread(here("data/zeal/Bzea_metadata.csv"))
pheno <- cly[!Plot %in% OUTLIER_PLOTS & !NIL %in% c("B73", "Purple Check") & !is.na(NIL)]
pheno[, accession := substr(NIL, 1, 7)]
pheno[, ancestry := meta$race[match(accession, meta$donor_id)]]
pheno <- merge(pheno, d250[, .(NIL, g250)], by = "NIL", all.x = TRUE)
pheno <- merge(pheno, d50[, .(NIL, g50, g50_3)], by = "NIL", all.x = TRUE)

# per-line table, restricted to lines carrying BOTH calls (fair head-to-head)
byNIL <- unique(pheno[!is.na(g250) & !is.na(g50), .(NIL, g250, g50, g50_3)], by = "NIL")
cat(sprintf(
  "lines with 250K call: %d | 50K call: %d | BOTH: %d\n",
  uniqueN(d250$NIL), uniqueN(d50$NIL), nrow(byNIL)
))


lines with 250K call: 900 | 50K call: 1394 | BOTH: 886

title: Inv4m tagging-SNP genotype: 50K vs 250K RTIGER calls, ZEAL NIL panel
text:
1. Confusion matrix, 50K (current) vs 250K (previous)
The 250K set resolves only presence/absence (recurrent / donor), while the 50K set resolves all three genotypes. A cell is a match when the two sets agree on presence: REF ↔︎ recurrent, and HET or ALT ↔︎ donor. Match cells are filled with the 50K genotype colour (state_palette); mismatches are white.


Show code
cm <- CJ(
  g50_3 = factor(c("REF", "HET", "ALT"), c("REF", "HET", "ALT")),
  g250 = factor(c("recurrent", "donor"), c("recurrent", "donor"))
)
cnt <- byNIL[, .N, by = .(g50_3, g250)]
cm <- cnt[cm, on = .(g50_3, g250)][is.na(N), N := 0L]
cm[, match := (g50_3 == "REF" & g250 == "recurrent") | (g50_3 %in% c("HET", "ALT") & g250 == "donor")]
cm[, fillkey := ifelse(match, as.character(g50_3), "mismatch")]
cm[, g50_3 := factor(g50_3, levels = c("ALT", "HET", "REF"))] # ALT top → REF bottom

n_tot <- sum(cm$N)
n_match <- cm[match == TRUE, sum(N)]
fill_vals <- c(geno3, mismatch = "white")

p_cm <- ggplot(cm, aes(g250, g50_3, fill = fillkey)) +
  geom_tile(color = "grey70", linewidth = 0.8) +
  # match-cell counts in white; mismatch-cell counts in grey for legibility on white
  geom_text(data = cm[match == TRUE], aes(label = N), color = "white", size = 7, fontface = "bold") +
  geom_text(data = cm[match == FALSE], aes(label = N), color = "grey35", size = 6) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = fill_vals, guide = "none") +
  scale_x_discrete(
    position = "top", limits = c("recurrent", "donor"),
    labels = c(recurrent = "B73\n(recurrent)", donor = "Introgression\n(donor)")
  ) +
  scale_y_discrete(labels = c(REF = "REF\n(recurrent)", HET = "HET", ALT = "ALT\n(hom. donor)")) +
  labs(
    x = "250K (previous), Nirwan",
    y = "50K (current)\nFausto",
    title = "Inv4m RTIGER calls",
    subtitle = sprintf(
      "%d NILs with both lines,\n%.1f%% concordant on presence",
      n_tot, 100 * n_match / n_tot
    )
  ) +
  coord_equal() +
  theme_classic2(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.line = element_blank(), axis.ticks = element_blank()
  )
p_cm











Show code
dcast(cm, g50_3 ~ g250, value.var = "N")[order(-match(g50_3, c("REF", "HET", "ALT")))] |>
  kable(caption = "NIL counts, 50K genotype (rows) × 250K genotype (columns)")

Reading it. The two RTIGER call sets agree on donor presence for 98.4% of lines. Where the 250K set calls Introgression, the 50K set resolves the zygosity into heterozygous (the majority) and homozygous-donor. The few off-diagonal lines are small, roughly symmetric disagreements at the detection margin, expected for two callers built on different reference variant sets.

title: Inv4m tagging-SNP genotype: 50K vs 250K RTIGER calls, ZEAL NIL panel
section: 2. Genotype frequency vs the single-locus BC2S3 expectation
text:
2. Genotype frequency vs the single-locus BC2S3 expectation






NoteBC2S3 Mendelian expectation at an unselected locus



Two backcrosses to B73 then three selfing generations. With no selection, donor-allele frequency is \(q = 1/8\) and heterozygosity \(1/4\) at BC2; three selfing generations shrink heterozygosity by \((1/2)^3\) while \(q\) is conserved:
\[
\Pr(Aa) = \tfrac14\left(\tfrac12\right)^3 = \tfrac{2}{64},\quad
\Pr(aa) = q - \tfrac12\Pr(Aa) = \tfrac{7}{64},\quad
\Pr(AA) = \tfrac{55}{64}.
\]
So an unselected BC2S3 locus is 85.9 % hom. recurrent, 3.1 % het, 10.9 % hom. donor, i.e. 14.1 % donor-carrying. This single-locus expectation is the null both call sets are tested against.




Show code
p_AA <- 55 / 64
p_Aa <- 2 / 64
p_aa <- 7 / 64
exp2 <- c(recurrent = p_AA, donor = p_Aa + p_aa)
exp3 <- c(REF = p_AA, HET = p_Aa, ALT = p_aa)

N <- nrow(byNIL)
o250 <- c(recurrent = byNIL[g250 == "recurrent", .N], donor = byNIL[g250 == "donor", .N])
o50 <- c(recurrent = byNIL[g50 == "recurrent", .N], donor = byNIL[g50 == "donor", .N])
o50_3 <- c(REF = byNIL[g50_3 == "REF", .N], HET = byNIL[g50_3 == "HET", .N], ALT = byNIL[g50_3 == "ALT", .N])



Stacked bar, both call sets alongside the BC2S3 expectation
Genotype-frequency spectrum in the REF / HET / ALT categories for all three groups. The 250K set does not resolve zygosity, so its Introgression calls are shown as homozygous ALT by assumption (it emits no heterozygotes); the 50K set carries its measured het / hom split.


Show code
o250_3 <- c(REF = unname(o250["recurrent"]), HET = 0, ALT = unname(o250["donor"])) # 250K donor := hom ALT
bar3 <- rbindlist(list(
  data.table(source = "250K\n(previous)", geno = names(o250_3), freq = o250_3 / N),
  data.table(source = "50K\n(current)", geno = names(o50_3), freq = o50_3 / N),
  data.table(source = "BC2S3\nexpected", geno = names(exp3), freq = exp3)
))
bar3[, geno := factor(geno, c("REF", "HET", "ALT"))]
bar3[, source := factor(source, c("250K\n(previous)", "50K\n(current)", "BC2S3\nexpected"))]

# donor-carrying total (HET + ALT) labelled atop each bar
donor_lab <- bar3[geno %in% c("HET", "ALT"), .(freq = sum(freq)), by = source]

ggplot(bar3, aes(source, freq, fill = geno)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.72, color = "white", linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_text(
    data = donor_lab, aes(x = source, y = freq, label = percent(freq, accuracy = 0.1)),
    vjust = -0.4, size = 4, fontface = "bold", inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = geno3, name = "Genotype",
    labels = c(REF = "hom. recurrent", HET = "heterozygous", ALT = "hom. donor")
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = percent, expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.08))) +
  labs(
    x = NULL, y = "Fraction of NIL lines",
    title = "Genotype-frequency spectrum at the Inv4m tagging SNP",
    subtitle = sprintf("n = %d NILs (both calls) · label = donor-carrying %% (HET + ALT) · BC2S3 donor = 14.1%%", N)
  ) +
  theme_classic2(base_size = 13) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"), legend.position = "bottom")











χ² goodness-of-fit tests


Show code
gof <- function(obs, p, label) {
  tt <- suppressWarnings(chisq.test(x = obs, p = p))
  data.table(
    comparison = label, classes = length(obs),
    X2 = unname(tt$statistic), df = unname(tt$parameter), p_value = tt$p.value
  )
}
rbindlist(list(
  gof(o250, exp2, "250K (previous) vs BC2S3   (2-class)"),
  gof(o50, exp2, "50K (current)   vs BC2S3   (2-class)"),
  gof(o50_3, exp3, "50K (current)   vs BC2S3   (3-class)")
)) |>
  mutate(p_value = format.pval(p_value, digits = 3, eps = 1e-300)) |>
  kable(digits = 1, caption = "χ² goodness-of-fit of each genotype spectrum against the single-locus BC2S3 expectation")

Reading it. On donor presence both call sets sit just below the 14.1 % BC2S3 expectation and track each other closely (250K ≈ 11.5 %, 50K ≈ 12.2 %); the 50K set is not significantly different from Mendelian expectation, the 250K only mildly so. The 3-class test is strongly rejected because the 50K carriers skew heterozygous (HET excess, hom-donor deficit vs three generations of selfing), consistent with introgressions maintained heterozygous rather than selfed to fixation, plus RTIGER’s tendency to call low-coverage / boundary donor tracts as heterozygous at ~0.4× skim.

title: Inv4m tagging-SNP genotype: 50K vs 250K RTIGER calls, ZEAL NIL panel
section: 3. Does the flowering effect change? (LMM refit)
text:
3. Does the flowering effect change? (LMM refit)
The paper’s model, refit with each genotype source. Both apply the paper’s both-arms-at-BC1 family filter on their own genotype definition.


Show code
fit_trait <- function(dd, trait, geno_col) {
  dd <- copy(dd)
  dd[, Inv4m := factor(get(geno_col), levels = c("recurrent", "donor"))]
  dd[, bc1 := substr(NIL, 1, 13)]
  dd[, bc2 := substr(NIL, 1, 16)]
  arms <- dd[!is.na(Inv4m), uniqueN(Inv4m), by = bc1]
  dd <- dd[bc1 %in% arms[V1 == 2, bc1] & !is.na(Inv4m) & !is.na(get(trait))]
  m <- suppressMessages(lmerTest::lmer(
    as.formula(paste(trait, "~ Inv4m + (1 | ancestry/bc2/NIL)")),
    data = dd
  ))
  fe <- summary(m)$coefficients["Inv4mdonor", ]
  data.table(
    genotype = geno_col, trait = trait,
    n_plots = nrow(dd), n_bc1_families = uniqueN(dd$bc1),
    est = fe["Estimate"], se = fe["Std. Error"], p = fe["Pr(>|t|)"]
  )
}
TRAITS <- c("DTA_corr", "DTS_corr", "PH_corr")
lmm_tbl <- rbindlist(lapply(TRAITS, function(tr) {
  rbindlist(list(
    fit_trait(pheno[!is.na(g250)], tr, "g250"),
    fit_trait(pheno[!is.na(g50)], tr, "g50")
  ))
}))
lmm_tbl[, genotype := factor(genotype, c("g250", "g50"), c("250K (previous)", "50K (current)"))]
lmm_tbl[order(trait, genotype)] |>
  kable(
    digits = c(0, 0, 0, 0, 3, 3, 6),
    col.names = c(
      "Genotype", "Trait", "n plots", "n BC1 families",
      "Inv4m effect (d/cm)", "SE", "p"
    ),
    caption = "Inv4m (donor − recurrent) fixed effect: 250K vs 50K genotype"

title: Inv4m tagging-SNP genotype: 50K vs 250K RTIGER calls, ZEAL NIL panel
text:
Takeaways

Concordance (confusion matrix). The 250K (previous) and 50K (current) RTIGER call sets agree on donor presence at the tagging SNP for ~98 % of lines; the 50K set additionally resolves the 250K Introgression state into heterozygous (majority) and homozygous-donor.
BC2S3 fit. Both sets recover a donor-carrying frequency (≈11,12 %) close to the single-locus BC2S3 expectation (14.1 %); the 50K set is statistically indistinguishable from it on presence. The 3-class spectrum departs from the strict selfing null through a heterozygote excess.
Flowering effect (LMM). The donor → earlier-flowering effect (DTS, DTA) is stable in direction and magnitude across both genotype sets; plant height stays non-significant. The paper’s conclusion does not depend on which RTIGER marker set defines the genotype.

title: ZEAL/BZea DTA GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — FDR ≤ 0.05
text:
What this is. The ZEAL/BZea analog of teonam-qtl-recovery-dta-mlm-118k, built from real BZea data end-to-end: field DTA → SpATS spatial BLUEs → SNP50K teosinte-ancestry dosage → RTIGER ancestry mosaic → K → JLM + MLM. The population is 1,403 near-isogenic B73×teosinte BC2S3 lines from 5 teosinte taxa (Zx mexicana 573, Zv parviglumis 383, Zd diploperennis 254, Zl luxurians 118, Zh huehuetenangensis 61) — the analog of TeoNAM’s 5 TIL families. The DTA BLUE is the per-field SpATS mean across two fields (CLY23-D4, CLY25-B5; cross-year genetic corr r = 0.675).
The central result mirrors TeoNAM: K = teosinte-ancestry relatedness is collinear with the flowering QTL, so the mixed model absorbs the very signal it protects — the flowering candidates (ZmCCT10, ZCN8) are suppressed under MLM. As in Chen 2019, the DTA QTL are therefore the province of JLM (nested-in-family stepwise), not the MLM Manhattan.
Show code
library(here)
library(data.table)
library(ggplot2)
library(fastman)
library(ggrepel)
library(ggtext)
library(scales)
library(cowplot)
source(here("scripts/teonam_notebook_plots.R")) # shared: plot_manhattan/lollipop, lambda_gc, LOD
Show code
mlm <- fread(here("data/zeal/dta_gwas_mlm_rtiger_mosaic_donor_accession_snp50k.csv")) # Panel C
gt <- fread(here("data/zeal/dta_gwas_mlm_hwe_post_gt_donor_accession_snp50k.csv")) # Panel B
ols <- fread(here("data/zeal/dta_gwas_ols_donor_accession_snp50k.csv"))[is.finite(P) & P > 0]
overlap <- here("results/sim/zeal/dta_candidate_overlap.csv")
lam <- function(p) {
  p <- p[is.finite(p) & p > 0 & p <= 1]
  round(median(qchisq(p, 1, lower.tail = FALSE)) / qchisq(0.5, 1), 2)
}
ALPHA <- 0.05
# -log10P threshold at Benjamini-Hochberg FDR <= ALPHA (NA if no marker passes)
fdr_lod <- function(p, alpha = ALPHA) {
  p <- p[is.finite(p) & p > 0]
  q <- p.adjust(p, "BH")
  if (!any(q <= alpha)) {
    return(NA_real_)
  }
  -log10(max(p[q <= alpha]))
}
qval <- function(p) p.adjust(p, "BH") # BH q-values aligned to p

title: ZEAL/BZea DTA GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — FDR ≤ 0.05
section: Candidate genes: closest marker vs window-max marker
text:
Candidate genes: closest marker vs window-max marker
For each maize flowering candidate (B73 v5), the closest marker to the gene body vs the highest −log₁₀P marker within ±500 kb in the RTIGER-mosaic MLM (Taxon+K) scan (Panel C). The same v5 flowering candidates as TeoNAM (these are maize genes; teosinte introgression at them drives DTA in both populations).


Show code
sc <- copy(mlm)[is.finite(P) & P > 0][, logP := -log10(P)][, q := qval(P)]
gg <- fread(here("data/teonam/dta_candidate_genes.tsv"))
gg[, chr := as.integer(chr)][, qtl := ifelse(qtl_chen2019 == "" | is.na(qtl_chen2019), paste0("DTA(chr", chr, ")"), qtl_chen2019)]
WIN <- 5e5
ctab <- rbindlist(lapply(seq_len(nrow(gg)), function(i) {
  ch <- gg$chr[i]
  s <- gg$start[i]
  e <- gg$end[i]
  m <- sc[CHR == ch]
  if (!nrow(m)) {
    return(NULL)
  }
  d <- pmax(0L, pmax(s - m$BP, m$BP - e))
  kc <- which.min(d)
  w <- m[BP >= s - WIN & BP <= e + WIN]
  kw <- if (nrow(w)) which.max(w$logP) else NA
  data.table(
    gene = gg$symbol[i], QTL = gg$qtl[i], chr = ch, `gene start (v5)` = s,
    `closest Δstart (bp)` = m$BP[kc] - s, `closest -log10P` = round(m$logP[kc], 2),
    `window-max -log10P` = if (nrow(w)) round(w$logP[kw], 2) else NA_real_,
    `window-max q (BH)` = if (nrow(w)) signif(w$q[kw], 2) else NA_real_,
    `FDR<=0.05` = if (nrow(w)) w$q[kw] <= ALPHA else NA
  )
}))
setorder(ctab, chr, `gene start (v5)`)
knitr::kable(ctab,
  format.args = list(big.mark = ","),
  caption = "DTA candidates (B73 v5): closest vs window-max (±500 kb) marker, RTIGER-mosaic MLM (Taxon+K) scan, with Benjamini–Hochberg q at the window-max marker."
)

title: ZEAL/BZea DTA GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — FDR ≤ 0.05
section: OLS → MLM: the flowering confound and structure control
text:
OLS → MLM: the flowering confound and structure control
The fixed-effect OLS (Taxon + marker, no K) vs the MLM (Taxon + K) on the RTIGER mosaic, same fixed part. OLS is intrinsically inflated (ancestry-block LD, unfixable by any fixed factor); K deflates λ toward 1 and collapses the flowering peaks (ZmCCT10), the ancestry-QTL confound.


Show code
plot_manhattan(ols,
  sprintf("ZEAL/BZea DTA — OLS (Taxon + marker, no K), RTIGER mosaic (λ_GC = %.2f; dotted = FDR 0.05)", lam(ols$P)),
  overlap_csv = overlap, lod = fdr_lod(ols$P),
  out_png = here("results/sim/zeal/dta_gwas_ols_manhattan_fdr_snp50k.png")
)









The OLS Manhattan (no K) is genome-wide inflated (λ_GC ≈ 2.4, ancestry-block LD) — many markers cross even the FDR line, and ZmCCT10 is nominally strong — precisely the structure artifact the MLM K term removes below.


Show code
lam_tab <- data.table(
  model = c("OLS (Taxon + marker, no K)", "MLM (Taxon + K)"),
  markers = c(nrow(ols), nrow(mlm)),
  lambda_GC = c(lam(ols$P), lam(mlm$P)),
  `n FDR<=0.05` = c(sum(qval(ols$P) <= ALPHA), sum(qval(mlm$P) <= ALPHA)),
  max_logP = round(c(max(-log10(ols$P)), max(-log10(mlm$P))), 2)
)
knitr::kable(lam_tab, caption = "OLS vs MLM on the RTIGER mosaic (same Taxon fixed part): K deflates genomic inflation; n markers at BH FDR ≤ 0.05.")

title: ZEAL/BZea DTA GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — FDR ≤ 0.05
text:
JLM QTL genomic distribution (Panel A)
DTA QTL from JLM (TASSEL StepwiseOLS, nested in taxon, enter/exit P=1e-4=LOD4). JLM QTL are selected by the stepwise enter/exit P (a model-selection gate), independent of the per-scan FDR line used for the GWAS Manhattans below. Model-independent — Chen’s primary DTA analysis.

)

title: ZEAL/BZea DTA GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — FDR ≤ 0.05
section: Composite (A: JLM · B: per-SNP MLM · C: RTIGER-mosaic MLM)
text:
Composite (A: JLM · B: per-SNP MLM · C: RTIGER-mosaic MLM)
Panel B (per-SNP genotype (bcftools HWE-posterior)) is λ_GC-deflated — the 70%-missing per-SNP panel at 0.4× is sparse — but catches sharp SNP hits (ZCN8) the mosaic block-terraces away. Panel C (the ancestry mosaic) is the usable, well-calibrated genotype. The B↔︎C contrast is the notebook’s per-SNP-scatter vs ancestry-block-smoothing, and the ZEAL demonstration that ancestry inference is mandatory at low coverage.


Show code
p_B <- plot_manhattan(gt,
  sprintf("DTA — MLM (Taxon+K), per-SNP genotype (bcftools HWE-posterior) (λ_GC = %.2f; dotted = FDR 0.05)", lam(gt$P)),
  overlap_csv = overlap, lod = fdr_lod(gt$P)
)
p_C <- plot_manhattan(mlm,
  sprintf("DTA — MLM (Taxon+K), RTIGER ancestry mosaic (λ_GC = %.2f; dotted = FDR 0.05)", lam(mlm$P)),
  overlap_csv = overlap, lod = fdr_lod(mlm$P)
)
composite <- cowplot::plot_grid(p_lolli, p_B, p_C, ncol = 1, labels = c("A", "B", "C"), label_size = 20, align = "v", axis = "lr")
ggsave(here("results/sim/zeal/dta_composite_snp50k.png"), composite, width = 9, height = 13, dpi = 200, bg = "white")
composite

title: ZEAL/BZea DTA GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — FDR ≤ 0.05
section: Multi-caller DTA scan (control · nNIL · RTIGER · LB-Impute · binhmm)
text:
Multi-caller DTA scan (control · nNIL · RTIGER · LB-Impute · binhmm)
The ZEAL analog of the TeoNAM DTA five-caller composite: the same DTA MLM (Taxon+K) scan on each ancestry caller’s recovered genotypes, at the real ~0.4× depth (not a coverage sweep). The scientific content here is caller reliability at low coverage: the ancestry mosaic must be block/bin-aggregated — the two aggregated callers (RTIGER per-donor segments; binhmm 1 Mb bins) recover the correct teosinte fraction (~0.10, matching the established BzeaSeq), while per-SNP calling (control) and the fresh per-SNP HMM recalls (nNIL, LB-Impute) under-call and give λ-deflated scans.


Show code
callers <- c(control = "hwe_post_gt", nNIL = "nnil_mosaic", RTIGER = "rtiger_mosaic", `LB-Impute` = "lbimpute_mosaic", binhmm = "binhmm_mosaic")
tb1 <- fread(here("data/teonam/dta_candidate_genes.tsv"))[symbol == "zmcct10"]
ctab2 <- rbindlist(lapply(names(callers), function(nm) {
  g <- callers[[nm]]
  sc <- fread(here(sprintf("data/zeal/dta_gwas_mlm_%s_donor_accession_snp50k.csv", g)))
  mf <- here(sprintf("data/zeal/zeal_%s.rds", g))
  pres <- if (file.exists(mf)) {
    st <- readRDS(mf)$state
    round(mean(st %in% c(1L, 2L), na.rm = TRUE), 3)
  } else {
    NA_real_
  }
  sc <- sc[is.finite(P) & P > 0]
  z <- sc[CHR == tb1$chr & abs(BP - tb1$start) <= 5e5]
  data.table(
    caller = nm, `teosinte presence` = pres, lambda_GC = lam(sc$P),
    `n FDR<=0.05` = sum(qval(sc$P) <= ALPHA),
    `zmcct10 q (BH)` = if (nrow(z)) signif(min(qval(sc$P)[sc$CHR == tb1$chr & abs(sc$BP - tb1$start) <= 5e5]), 2) else NA_real_
  )
}))
knitr::kable(ctab2, caption = "DTA MLM (Taxon+K) by ancestry caller at 0.4×, with n markers at BH FDR ≤ 0.05 and the zmcct10 q. Aggregated callers (RTIGER, binhmm) recover teosinte presence ~0.10; per-SNP (control) and per-SNP recalls (nNIL, LB-Impute) under-call and deflate λ_GC.")

title: ZEAL/BZea DTA (days to anthesis) — MLM (Taxon + K) GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel
text:
What this is. The ZEAL/BZea analog of teonam-qtl-recovery-dta-mlm-118k, built from real BZea data end-to-end: field DTA → SpATS spatial BLUEs → SNP50K teosinte-ancestry dosage → RTIGER ancestry mosaic → K → JLM + MLM. The population is 1,403 near-isogenic B73×teosinte BC2S3 lines from 5 teosinte taxa (Zx mexicana 573, Zv parviglumis 383, Zd diploperennis 254, Zl luxurians 118, Zh huehuetenangensis 61) — the analog of TeoNAM’s 5 TIL families. The DTA BLUE is the per-field SpATS mean across two fields (CLY23-D4, CLY25-B5; cross-year genetic corr r = 0.675).
The central result mirrors TeoNAM: K = teosinte-ancestry relatedness is collinear with the flowering QTL, so the mixed model absorbs the very signal it protects — the flowering candidates (ZmCCT10, ZCN8) are suppressed under MLM. As in Chen 2019, the DTA QTL are therefore the province of JLM (nested-in-family stepwise), not the MLM Manhattan.
Show code
library(here)
library(data.table)
library(ggplot2)
library(fastman)
library(ggrepel)
library(ggtext)
library(scales)
library(cowplot)
source(here("scripts/teonam_notebook_plots.R")) # shared: plot_manhattan/lollipop, lambda_gc, LOD
Show code
mlm <- fread(here("data/zeal/dta_gwas_mlm_rtiger_mosaic_donor_accession_snp50k.csv")) # Panel C
gt <- fread(here("data/zeal/dta_gwas_mlm_hwe_post_gt_donor_accession_snp50k.csv")) # Panel B
ols <- fread(here("data/zeal/dta_gwas_ols_donor_accession_snp50k.csv"))[is.finite(P) & P > 0]
overlap <- here("results/sim/zeal/dta_candidate_overlap.csv")
lam <- function(p) {
  p <- p[is.finite(p) & p > 0 & p <= 1]
  round(median(qchisq(p, 1, lower.tail = FALSE)) / qchisq(0.5, 1), 2)
}

title: ZEAL/BZea DTA (days to anthesis) — MLM (Taxon + K) GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel
section: Candidate genes: closest marker vs window-max marker
text:
Candidate genes: closest marker vs window-max marker
For each maize flowering candidate (B73 v5), the closest marker to the gene body vs the highest −log₁₀P marker within ±500 kb in the RTIGER-mosaic MLM (Taxon+K) scan (Panel C). The same v5 flowering candidates as TeoNAM (these are maize genes; teosinte introgression at them drives DTA in both populations).


Show code
sc <- copy(mlm)[is.finite(P) & P > 0][, logP := -log10(P)]
gg <- fread(here("data/teonam/dta_candidate_genes.tsv"))
gg[, chr := as.integer(chr)][, qtl := ifelse(qtl_chen2019 == "" | is.na(qtl_chen2019), paste0("DTA(chr", chr, ")"), qtl_chen2019)]
WIN <- 5e5
ctab <- rbindlist(lapply(seq_len(nrow(gg)), function(i) {
  ch <- gg$chr[i]
  s <- gg$start[i]
  e <- gg$end[i]
  m <- sc[CHR == ch]
  if (!nrow(m)) {
    return(NULL)
  }
  d <- pmax(0L, pmax(s - m$BP, m$BP - e))
  kc <- which.min(d)
  w <- m[BP >= s - WIN & BP <= e + WIN]
  kw <- if (nrow(w)) which.max(w$logP) else NA
  data.table(
    gene = gg$symbol[i], QTL = gg$qtl[i], chr = ch, `gene start (v5)` = s,
    `closest Δstart (bp)` = m$BP[kc] - s, `closest -log10P` = round(m$logP[kc], 2),
    `window-max -log10P` = if (nrow(w)) round(w$logP[kw], 2) else NA_real_
  )
}))
setorder(ctab, chr, `gene start (v5)`)
knitr::kable(ctab,
  format.args = list(big.mark = ","),
  caption = "DTA candidates (B73 v5): closest vs window-max (±500 kb) marker, RTIGER-mosaic MLM (Taxon+K) scan."
)

  z <- sc[CHR == tb1$chr & abs(BP - tb1$start) <= 5e5 & is.finite(P) & P > 0]
  data.table(
    caller = nm, `teosinte presence` = pres, lambda_GC = lam(sc$P),
    `max -log10P` = round(max(-log10(sc[is.finite(P) & P > 0, P])), 2),
    `zmcct10 -log10P` = if (nrow(z)) round(max(-log10(z$P)), 2) else NA_real_
  )
}))

title: ZEAL/BZea DTS GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — FDR ≤ 0.05
text:
What this is. The days-to-silk (DTS) sibling of zeal-qtl-recovery-dta-mlm-snp50k-fdr, built from the same real BZea pipeline: field DTS → SpATS spatial BLUEs → SNP50K teosinte-ancestry dosage → RTIGER ancestry mosaic → K → JLM + MLM. The population is the same 1,403 near-isogenic B73×teosinte BC2S3 lines from 5 teosinte taxa (Zx 578, Zv 385, Zd 255, Zl 118, Zh 61). The DTS BLUE is the per-field SpATS mean across two fields (CLY23-D4, CLY25-B5); union mean ≈ 82 ± 4 days. DTS is a flowering trait, essentially the female counterpart of DTA (anthesis): the two are tightly genetically correlated, so the candidate genes and the ancestry-flowering confound below are identical to the DTA notebook — only the phenotype changes.
As in DTA, K = teosinte-ancestry relatedness is collinear with the flowering QTL, so the mixed model absorbs the very signal it protects — the flowering candidates (ZmCCT10, ZCN8) are suppressed under MLM. The DTS QTL are therefore the province of JLM (nested-in-family stepwise), not the MLM Manhattan.
Show code
library(here)
library(data.table)
library(ggplot2)
library(fastman)
library(ggrepel)
library(ggtext)
library(scales)
library(cowplot)
source(here("scripts/teonam_notebook_plots.R")) # shared: plot_manhattan/lollipop, lambda_gc, LOD
Show code
mlm <- fread(here("data/zeal/dts_gwas_mlm_rtiger_mosaic_donor_accession_snp50k.csv")) # Panel C
gt <- fread(here("data/zeal/dts_gwas_mlm_hwe_post_gt_donor_accession_snp50k.csv")) # Panel B
ols <- fread(here("data/zeal/dts_gwas_ols_donor_accession_snp50k.csv"))[is.finite(P) & P > 0]
overlap <- here("results/sim/zeal/dts_candidate_overlap.csv")
lam <- function(p) {
  p <- p[is.finite(p) & p > 0 & p <= 1]
  round(median(qchisq(p, 1, lower.tail = FALSE)) / qchisq(0.5, 1), 2)
}
ALPHA <- 0.05
fdr_lod <- function(p, alpha = ALPHA) {
  p <- p[is.finite(p) & p > 0]
  q <- p.adjust(p, "BH")
  if (!any(q <= alpha)) {
    return(NA_real_)
  }
  -log10(max(p[q <= alpha]))
}
qval <- function(p) p.adjust(p, "BH") # BH q-values aligned to p

title: ZEAL/BZea DTS GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — FDR ≤ 0.05
section: Candidate genes: closest marker vs window-max marker
text:
Candidate genes: closest marker vs window-max marker
For each maize flowering candidate (B73 v5), the closest marker to the gene body vs the highest −log₁₀P marker within ±500 kb in the RTIGER-mosaic MLM (Taxon+K) scan (Panel C). The same v5 flowering candidates as DTA (these are maize genes; teosinte introgression at them drives flowering time in both sexes).


Show code
sc <- copy(mlm)[is.finite(P) & P > 0][, logP := -log10(P)][, q := qval(P)]
gg <- fread(here("data/teonam/dts_candidate_genes.tsv"))
gg[, chr := as.integer(chr)][, qtl := ifelse(qtl_chen2019 == "" | is.na(qtl_chen2019), paste0("DTS(chr", chr, ")"), qtl_chen2019)]
WIN <- 5e5
ctab <- rbindlist(lapply(seq_len(nrow(gg)), function(i) {
  ch <- gg$chr[i]
  s <- gg$start[i]
  e <- gg$end[i]
  m <- sc[CHR == ch]
  if (!nrow(m)) {
    return(NULL)
  }
  d <- pmax(0L, pmax(s - m$BP, m$BP - e))
  kc <- which.min(d)
  w <- m[BP >= s - WIN & BP <= e + WIN]
  kw <- if (nrow(w)) which.max(w$logP) else NA
  data.table(
    gene = gg$symbol[i], QTL = gg$qtl[i], chr = ch, `gene start (v5)` = s,
    `closest Δstart (bp)` = m$BP[kc] - s, `closest -log10P` = round(m$logP[kc], 2),
    `window-max -log10P` = if (nrow(w)) round(w$logP[kw], 2) else NA_real_,
    `window-max q (BH)` = if (nrow(w)) signif(w$q[kw], 2) else NA_real_,
    `FDR<=0.05` = if (nrow(w)) w$q[kw] <= ALPHA else NA
  )
}))
setorder(ctab, chr, `gene start (v5)`)
knitr::kable(ctab,
  format.args = list(big.mark = ","),
  caption = "DTS candidates (B73 v5): closest vs window-max (±500 kb) marker, RTIGER-mosaic MLM (Taxon+K) scan, with Benjamini–Hochberg q at the window-max marker."
)

title: ZEAL/BZea DTS GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — FDR ≤ 0.05
section: OLS → MLM: the flowering confound and structure control
text:
OLS → MLM: the flowering confound and structure control
The fixed-effect OLS (Taxon + marker, no K) vs the MLM (Taxon + K) on the RTIGER mosaic, same fixed part. OLS is intrinsically inflated (ancestry-block LD); K deflates λ toward 1 and collapses the flowering peaks (ZmCCT10), the ancestry-QTL confound.


Show code
plot_manhattan(ols,
  sprintf("ZEAL/BZea DTS — OLS (Taxon + marker, no K), RTIGER mosaic (λ_GC = %.2f; dotted = FDR 0.05)", lam(ols$P)),
  overlap_csv = overlap, lod = fdr_lod(ols$P),
  out_png = here("results/sim/zeal/dts_gwas_ols_manhattan_fdr_snp50k.png")
)











Show code
lam_tab <- data.table(
  model = c("OLS (Taxon + marker, no K)", "MLM (Taxon + K)"),
  markers = c(nrow(ols), nrow(mlm)),
  lambda_GC = c(lam(ols$P), lam(mlm$P)),
  `n FDR<=0.05` = c(sum(qval(ols$P) <= ALPHA), sum(qval(mlm$P) <= ALPHA)),
  max_logP = round(c(max(-log10(ols$P)), max(-log10(mlm$P))), 2)
)

title: ZEAL/BZea DTS GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — FDR ≤ 0.05
section: Composite (A: JLM · B: per-SNP MLM · C: RTIGER-mosaic MLM)
text:
Composite (A: JLM · B: per-SNP MLM · C: RTIGER-mosaic MLM)
Panel B (per-SNP genotype (bcftools HWE-posterior)) is λ_GC-deflated but catches sharp SNP hits (ZCN8) the mosaic block-terraces away. Panel C (the ancestry mosaic) is the usable, well-calibrated genotype. The B↔︎C contrast is the notebook’s per-SNP-scatter vs ancestry-block-smoothing.


Show code
p_B <- plot_manhattan(gt,
  sprintf("DTS — MLM (Taxon+K), per-SNP genotype (bcftools HWE-posterior) (λ_GC = %.2f)", lam(gt$P)),
  overlap_csv = overlap, lod = fdr_lod(gt$P)
)
p_C <- plot_manhattan(mlm,
  sprintf("DTS — MLM (Taxon+K), RTIGER ancestry mosaic (λ_GC = %.2f)", lam(mlm$P)),
  overlap_csv = overlap, lod = fdr_lod(mlm$P)
)
composite <- cowplot::plot_grid(p_lolli, p_B, p_C, ncol = 1, labels = c("A", "B", "C"), label_size = 20, align = "v", axis = "lr")
ggsave(here("results/sim/zeal/dts_composite_snp50k.png"), composite, width = 9, height = 13, dpi = 200, bg = "white")
composite

title: ZEAL/BZea DTS GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — FDR ≤ 0.05
section: Multi-caller DTS scan (control · nNIL · RTIGER · LB-Impute · binhmm)
text:
Multi-caller DTS scan (control · nNIL · RTIGER · LB-Impute · binhmm)
The same DTS MLM (Taxon+K) scan on each ancestry caller’s recovered genotypes, at the real ~0.4× depth. Caller reliability at low coverage: the block/bin-aggregated callers (RTIGER per-donor segments; binhmm 1 Mb bins) recover the correct teosinte fraction (~0.10), while per-SNP calling (control) and the fresh per-SNP HMM recalls (nNIL, LB-Impute) under-call and give λ-deflated scans. Caller genotypes are population-level and trait-independent; only the phenotype (DTS) changes.


Show code
callers <- c(control = "hwe_post_gt", nNIL = "nnil_mosaic", RTIGER = "rtiger_mosaic", `LB-Impute` = "lbimpute_mosaic", binhmm = "binhmm_mosaic")
tb1 <- fread(here("data/teonam/dts_candidate_genes.tsv"))[symbol == "zmcct10"]
ctab2 <- rbindlist(lapply(names(callers), function(nm) {
  g <- callers[[nm]]
  sc <- fread(here(sprintf("data/zeal/dts_gwas_mlm_%s_donor_accession_snp50k.csv", g)))
  mf <- here(sprintf("data/zeal/zeal_%s.rds", g))
  pres <- if (file.exists(mf)) {
    st <- readRDS(mf)$state
    round(mean(st %in% c(1L, 2L), na.rm = TRUE), 3)
  } else {
    NA_real_
  }
  sc <- sc[is.finite(P) & P > 0]
  z <- sc[CHR == tb1$chr & abs(BP - tb1$start) <= 5e5]
  data.table(
    caller = nm, `teosinte presence` = pres, lambda_GC = lam(sc$P),
    `n FDR<=0.05` = sum(qval(sc$P) <= ALPHA),
    `zmcct10 q (BH)` = if (nrow(z)) signif(min(qval(sc$P)[sc$CHR == tb1$chr & abs(sc$BP - tb1$start) <= 5e5]), 2) else NA_real_
  )
}))
knitr::kable(ctab2, caption = "DTS MLM (Taxon+K) by ancestry caller at 0.4×, with n markers at BH FDR ≤ 0.05 and the zmcct10 q. Aggregated callers (RTIGER, binhmm) recover teosinte presence ~0.10; per-SNP (control) and per-SNP recalls (nNIL, LB-Impute) under-call and deflate λ_GC.")

title: ZEAL/BZea Plant-Height (PH) GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — FDR ≤ 0.05
text:
What this is. The plant-height (PH) sibling of zeal-qtl-recovery-dta-mlm-snp50k-fdr, built from the same real BZea pipeline end-to-end: field PH → SpATS spatial BLUEs → SNP50K teosinte-ancestry dosage → RTIGER ancestry mosaic → K → JLM + MLM. The population is the same 1,403 near-isogenic B73×teosinte BC2S3 lines from 5 teosinte taxa (Zx mexicana 578, Zv parviglumis 385, Zd diploperennis 255, Zl luxurians 118, Zh huehuetenangensis 61). The PH BLUE is the per-field SpATS mean across two fields (CLY23-D4, CLY25-B5); union mean ≈ 183 ± 16 cm.
Trait context. Plant height is an agronomic/architecture trait, not a flowering trait. In TeoNAM (Chen 2019), PLHT is polygenic with only a few QTL of small effect and no single large-effect locus — the opposite of the DTA/flowering architecture that had ZmCCT10/ZCN8. The height candidates here are therefore the classic maize dwarf / gibberellin (GA) / brassinosteroid (BR) / auxin genes named in Peiffer et al. 2014, The Genetic Architecture of Maize Height (Genetics 196:1337) — br1, br2 (internode/auxin), an1, d1, d3, d9, gid1 (GA), brd1, na2 (BR), d8 (DELLA/GA signaling). Teosinte is taller and more branched than B73, so teosinte ancestry is broadly height-increasing and partly collinear with K; as in DTA, the mixed model absorbs ancestry-correlated variance, and the model-independent JLM (nested-in-family stepwise) is the primary QTL tool.
Show code
library(here)
library(data.table)
library(ggplot2)
library(fastman)
library(ggrepel)
library(ggtext)
library(scales)
library(cowplot)
source(here("scripts/teonam_notebook_plots.R")) # shared: plot_manhattan/lollipop, lambda_gc, LOD
Show code
mlm <- fread(here("data/zeal/ph_gwas_mlm_rtiger_mosaic_donor_accession_snp50k.csv")) # Panel C
gt <- fread(here("data/zeal/ph_gwas_mlm_hwe_post_gt_donor_accession_snp50k.csv")) # Panel B
ols <- fread(here("data/zeal/ph_gwas_ols_donor_accession_snp50k.csv"))[is.finite(P) & P > 0]
overlap <- here("results/sim/zeal/ph_candidate_overlap.csv")
lam <- function(p) {
  p <- p[is.finite(p) & p > 0 & p <= 1]
  round(median(qchisq(p, 1, lower.tail = FALSE)) / qchisq(0.5, 1), 2)
}
ALPHA <- 0.05
# -log10P threshold at Benjamini-Hochberg FDR <= ALPHA (NA if no marker passes)
fdr_lod <- function(p, alpha = ALPHA) {
  p <- p[is.finite(p) & p > 0]
  q <- p.adjust(p, "BH")
  if (!any(q <= alpha)) {
    return(NA_real_)
  }
  -log10(max(p[q <= alpha]))
}
qval <- function(p) p.adjust(p, "BH") # BH q-values aligned to p

title: ZEAL/BZea Plant-Height (PH) GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — FDR ≤ 0.05
section: Candidate genes: closest marker vs window-max marker
text:
Candidate genes: closest marker vs window-max marker
For each maize plant-height candidate (B73 v5), the closest marker to the gene body vs the highest −log₁₀P marker within ±500 kb in the RTIGER-mosaic MLM (Taxon+K) scan (Panel C). Candidates are the dwarf/GA/BR/auxin height genes from Peiffer et al. 2014 (v5 coordinates); pathway records the hormone/growth pathway each acts in.


Show code
sc <- copy(mlm)[is.finite(P) & P > 0][, logP := -log10(P)][, q := qval(P)]
gg <- fread(here("data/teonam/ph_candidate_genes.tsv"))
gg[, chr := as.integer(chr)][, qtl := ifelse(qtl_chen2019 == "" | is.na(qtl_chen2019), paste0("PH(chr", chr, ")"), qtl_chen2019)]
WIN <- 5e5
ctab <- rbindlist(lapply(seq_len(nrow(gg)), function(i) {
  ch <- gg$chr[i]
  s <- gg$start[i]
  e <- gg$end[i]
  m <- sc[CHR == ch]
  if (!nrow(m)) {
    return(NULL)
  }
  d <- pmax(0L, pmax(s - m$BP, m$BP - e))
  kc <- which.min(d)
  w <- m[BP >= s - WIN & BP <= e + WIN]
  kw <- if (nrow(w)) which.max(w$logP) else NA
  data.table(
    gene = gg$symbol[i], pathway = gg$pathway[i], chr = ch, `gene start (v5)` = s,
    `closest Δstart (bp)` = m$BP[kc] - s, `closest -log10P` = round(m$logP[kc], 2),
    `window-max -log10P` = if (nrow(w)) round(w$logP[kw], 2) else NA_real_,
    `window-max q (BH)` = if (nrow(w)) signif(w$q[kw], 2) else NA_real_,
    `FDR<=0.05` = if (nrow(w)) w$q[kw] <= ALPHA else NA
  )
}))
setorder(ctab, chr, `gene start (v5)`)
knitr::kable(ctab,
  format.args = list(big.mark = ","),

title: ZEAL/BZea Plant-Height (PH) GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — FDR ≤ 0.05
section: OLS → MLM: structure control on a polygenic trait
text:
OLS → MLM: structure control on a polygenic trait
The fixed-effect OLS (Taxon + marker, no K) vs the MLM (Taxon + K) on the RTIGER mosaic, same fixed part. OLS is intrinsically inflated (ancestry-block LD, unfixable by any fixed factor); K deflates λ toward 1 and removes the ancestry-correlated bulk that a near-isogenic, teosinte-ancestry-structured height panel carries.


Show code
plot_manhattan(ols,
  sprintf("ZEAL/BZea PH — OLS (Taxon + marker, no K), RTIGER mosaic (λ_GC = %.2f; dotted = FDR 0.05)", lam(ols$P)),
  overlap_csv = overlap, lod = fdr_lod(ols$P),
  out_png = here("results/sim/zeal/ph_gwas_ols_manhattan_fdr_snp50k.png")
)









The OLS Manhattan (no K) is genome-wide inflated (ancestry-block LD) — the same structure artifact the MLM K term removes below.


Show code
lam_tab <- data.table(
  model = c("OLS (Taxon + marker, no K)", "MLM (Taxon + K)"),
  markers = c(nrow(ols), nrow(mlm)),
  lambda_GC = c(lam(ols$P), lam(mlm$P)),
  `n FDR<=0.05` = c(sum(qval(ols$P) <= ALPHA), sum(qval(mlm$P) <= ALPHA)),
  max_logP = round(c(max(-log10(ols$P)), max(-log10(mlm$P))), 2)
)
knitr::kable(lam_tab, caption = "OLS vs MLM on the RTIGER mosaic (same Taxon fixed part): K deflates genomic inflation; n markers at BH FDR ≤ 0.05.")

JLM QTL genomic distribution (Panel A)
PH QTL from JLM (TASSEL StepwiseOLS, nested in taxon, enter/exit P=1e-4=LOD4). JLM QTL are selected by the stepwise enter/exit P (a model-selection gate), independent of the per-scan FDR line used for the GWAS Manhattans below. Model-independent — the primary PH analysis, as in Chen 2019.

title: ZEAL/BZea Plant-Height (PH) GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — FDR ≤ 0.05
section: Composite (A: JLM · B: per-SNP MLM · C: RTIGER-mosaic MLM)
text:
Composite (A: JLM · B: per-SNP MLM · C: RTIGER-mosaic MLM)
Panel B (per-SNP genotype (bcftools HWE-posterior)) is λ_GC-deflated — the 70%-missing per-SNP panel at 0.4× is sparse — but catches sharp SNP hits the mosaic block-terraces away. Panel C (the ancestry mosaic) is the usable, well-calibrated genotype. The B↔︎C contrast is the notebook’s per-SNP-scatter vs ancestry-block-smoothing, and the demonstration that ancestry inference is mandatory at low coverage — identical in mechanism to the DTA notebook.


Show code
p_B <- plot_manhattan(gt,
  sprintf("PH — MLM (Taxon+K), per-SNP genotype (bcftools HWE-posterior) (λ_GC = %.2f)", lam(gt$P)),
  overlap_csv = overlap, lod = fdr_lod(gt$P)
)
p_C <- plot_manhattan(mlm,
  sprintf("PH — MLM (Taxon+K), RTIGER ancestry mosaic (λ_GC = %.2f)", lam(mlm$P)),
  overlap_csv = overlap, lod = fdr_lod(mlm$P)
)
composite <- cowplot::plot_grid(p_lolli, p_B, p_C, ncol = 1, labels = c("A", "B", "C"), label_size = 20, align = "v", axis = "lr")
ggsave(here("results/sim/zeal/ph_composite_snp50k.png"), composite, width = 9, height = 13, dpi = 200, bg = "white")
composite

title: ZEAL/BZea Plant-Height (PH) GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — FDR ≤ 0.05
section: Multi-caller PH scan (control · nNIL · RTIGER · LB-Impute · binhmm)
text:
Multi-caller PH scan (control · nNIL · RTIGER · LB-Impute · binhmm)
The same PH MLM (Taxon+K) scan on each ancestry caller’s recovered genotypes, at the real ~0.4× depth (not a coverage sweep). The scientific content is caller reliability at low coverage. The robust, trait-independent finding is teosinte-presence recovery: the two block/bin-aggregated callers (RTIGER per-donor segments 0.111; binhmm 1 Mb bins 0.101) recover the established BzeaSeq fraction (~0.10), while per-SNP calling (control) and the fresh per-SNP HMM recalls (nNIL, LB-Impute) under-call. Genomic inflation, by contrast, is phenotype-dependent: for PH the per-SNP callers stay λ-deflated (control 0.79, LB-Impute 0.85), the RTIGER mosaic is the best-calibrated (λ ≈ 1.3), and binhmm actually runs inflated (λ ≈ 2.9, ~1,190 markers past FDR) — a caution that recovering the right ancestry fraction does not by itself guarantee a well-calibrated scan when residual ancestry structure aligns with a polygenic trait like height. The caller genotypes are population-level and trait-independent; only the phenotype (PH) changes vs the DTA notebook.


Show code
callers <- c(control = "hwe_post_gt", nNIL = "nnil_mosaic", RTIGER = "rtiger_mosaic", `LB-Impute` = "lbimpute_mosaic", binhmm = "binhmm_mosaic")
gg <- fread(here("data/teonam/ph_candidate_genes.tsv"))[, chr := as.integer(chr)]
# best (min-q) PH candidate window per caller — PH is polygenic, no single headline gene
min_cand_q <- function(sc) {
  q <- qval(sc$P)
  vals <- unlist(lapply(seq_len(nrow(gg)), function(i) {
    idx <- which(sc$CHR == gg$chr[i] & abs(sc$BP - gg$start[i]) <= 5e5)
    if (length(idx)) min(q[idx]) else NA_real_
  }))
  if (all(is.na(vals))) NA_real_ else signif(min(vals, na.rm = TRUE), 2)
}
ctab2 <- rbindlist(lapply(names(callers), function(nm) {
  g <- callers[[nm]]
  sc <- fread(here(sprintf("data/zeal/ph_gwas_mlm_%s_donor_accession_snp50k.csv", g)))
  mf <- here(sprintf("data/zeal/zeal_%s.rds", g))
  pres <- if (file.exists(mf)) {
    st <- readRDS(mf)$state
    round(mean(st %in% c(1L, 2L), na.rm = TRUE), 3)
  } else {
    NA_real_
  }
  sc <- sc[is.finite(P) & P > 0]
  data.table(
    caller = nm, `teosinte presence` = pres, lambda_GC = lam(sc$P),
    `n FDR<=0.05` = sum(qval(sc$P) <= ALPHA),
    `best candidate q (BH)` = min_cand_q(sc)
  )
}))
knitr::kable(ctab2, caption = "PH MLM (Taxon+K) by ancestry caller at 0.4×, with n markers at BH FDR ≤ 0.05 and the best (min) q across the PH candidate windows. Aggregated callers (RTIGER, binhmm) recover teosinte presence ~0.10; per-SNP (control) and per-SNP recalls (nNIL, LB-Impute) under-call and deflate λ_GC.")

title: ZEAL/BZea Stem-Pigment (StPi) GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — FDR ≤ 0.05
text:
What this is. The stem-pigment (StPi) sibling of zeal-qtl-recovery-dta-mlm-snp50k-fdr, built from the same real BZea pipeline: field StPi → per-genotype empirical logit → SNP50K teosinte-ancestry dosage → RTIGER ancestry mosaic → K → JLM + MLM. The population is the same near-isogenic B73×teosinte BC2S3 lines from 5 teosinte taxa. StPi is a binary 0/1 stem-pigment call per plot (pigmented / not); the per-genotype phenotype is the proportion of pigmented plots, k/n, and only ~22% of genotypes are ever pigmented.
Trait context. Stem pigmentation is an anthocyanin trait. In TeoNAM (Chen 2019) the pigment/color traits (e.g. glume color) had simple genetic architectures — a single large-effect QTL plus a few small-effect ones — unlike the polygenic flowering (DTA/DTS) or height (PH) traits. The candidates here are the classic maize anthocyanin-biosynthesis genes: the regulators r1/b1 (bHLH) and c1/pl1 (MYB) that switch the pathway on, and the structural enzymes c2 (CHS), chi1 (CHI), pr1 (F3’H), a1 (DFR), a2 (ANS), bz1 (UFGT) and bz2 (GST).
Show code
library(here)
library(data.table)
library(ggplot2)
library(fastman)
library(ggrepel)
library(ggtext)
library(scales)
library(cowplot)
source(here("scripts/teonam_notebook_plots.R")) # shared: plot_manhattan/lollipop, lambda_gc, LOD
Show code
mlm <- fread(here("data/zeal/stpi_gwas_mlm_rtiger_mosaic_donor_accession_snp50k.csv")) # Panel C
gt <- fread(here("data/zeal/stpi_gwas_mlm_hwe_post_gt_donor_accession_snp50k.csv")) # Panel B
ols <- fread(here("data/zeal/stpi_gwas_ols_donor_accession_snp50k.csv"))[is.finite(P) & P > 0]
overlap <- here("results/sim/zeal/stpi_candidate_overlap.csv")
lam <- function(p) {
  p <- p[is.finite(p) & p > 0 & p <= 1]
  round(median(qchisq(p, 1, lower.tail = FALSE)) / qchisq(0.5, 1), 2)
}
ALPHA <- 0.05
fdr_lod <- function(p, alpha = ALPHA) {
  p <- p[is.finite(p) & p > 0]
  q <- p.adjust(p, "BH")
  if (!any(q <= alpha)) {
    return(NA_real_)
  }
  -log10(max(p[q <= alpha]))
}
qval <- function(p) p.adjust(p, "BH") # BH q-values aligned to p

title: ZEAL/BZea Stem-Pigment (StPi) GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — FDR ≤ 0.05
section: Candidate genes: closest marker vs window-max marker
text:
Candidate genes: closest marker vs window-max marker
For each maize anthocyanin candidate (B73 v5), the closest marker to the gene body vs the highest −log₁₀P marker within ±500 kb in the RTIGER-mosaic MLM (Taxon+K) scan (Panel C). pathway records the biosynthetic role (regulatory bHLH/MYB vs structural enzyme).


Show code
sc <- copy(mlm)[is.finite(P) & P > 0][, logP := -log10(P)][, q := qval(P)]
gg <- fread(here("data/teonam/stpi_candidate_genes.tsv"))
gg[, chr := as.integer(chr)]
WIN <- 5e5
ctab <- rbindlist(lapply(seq_len(nrow(gg)), function(i) {
  ch <- gg$chr[i]
  s <- gg$start[i]
  e <- gg$end[i]
  m <- sc[CHR == ch]
  if (!nrow(m)) {
    return(NULL)
  }
  d <- pmax(0L, pmax(s - m$BP, m$BP - e))
  kc <- which.min(d)
  w <- m[BP >= s - WIN & BP <= e + WIN]
  kw <- if (nrow(w)) which.max(w$logP) else NA
  data.table(
    gene = gg$symbol[i], pathway = gg$pathway[i], chr = ch, `gene start (v5)` = s,
    `closest Δstart (bp)` = m$BP[kc] - s, `closest -log10P` = round(m$logP[kc], 2),
    `window-max -log10P` = if (nrow(w)) round(w$logP[kw], 2) else NA_real_,
    `window-max q (BH)` = if (nrow(w)) signif(w$q[kw], 2) else NA_real_,
    `FDR<=0.05` = if (nrow(w)) w$q[kw] <= ALPHA else NA
  )
}))
setorder(ctab, chr, `gene start (v5)`)
knitr::kable(ctab,
  format.args = list(big.mark = ","),
  caption = "StPi candidates (B73 v5, anthocyanin pathway): closest vs window-max (±500 kb) marker, RTIGER-mosaic MLM (Taxon+K) scan, with Benjamini–Hochberg q at the window-max marker."
)

title: ZEAL/BZea Stem-Pigment (StPi) GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — FDR ≤ 0.05
section: Composite (A: JLM · B: per-SNP MLM · C: RTIGER-mosaic MLM)
text:
Composite (A: JLM · B: per-SNP MLM · C: RTIGER-mosaic MLM)
Panel B (per-SNP genotype (bcftools HWE-posterior)) is λ_GC-deflated but catches sharp SNP hits the mosaic block-terraces away. Panel C (the ancestry mosaic) is the usable, well-calibrated genotype.


Show code
p_B <- plot_manhattan(gt,
  sprintf("StPi — MLM (Taxon+K), per-SNP genotype (bcftools HWE-posterior) (λ_GC = %.2f)", lam(gt$P)),
  overlap_csv = overlap, lod = fdr_lod(gt$P)
)
p_C <- plot_manhattan(mlm,
  sprintf("StPi — MLM (Taxon+K), RTIGER ancestry mosaic (λ_GC = %.2f)", lam(mlm$P)),
  overlap_csv = overlap, lod = fdr_lod(mlm$P)
)
composite <- cowplot::plot_grid(p_lolli, p_B, p_C, ncol = 1, labels = c("A", "B", "C"), label_size = 20, align = "v", axis = "lr")
ggsave(here("results/sim/zeal/stpi_composite_snp50k.png"), composite, width = 9, height = 13, dpi = 200, bg = "white")

title: ZEAL/BZea Stem-Pigment (StPi) GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — FDR ≤ 0.05
section: Multi-caller StPi scan (control · nNIL · RTIGER · LB-Impute · binhmm)
text:
Multi-caller StPi scan (control · nNIL · RTIGER · LB-Impute · binhmm)
The same StPi MLM (Taxon+K) scan on each ancestry caller’s recovered genotypes, at the real ~0.4× depth. The robust, trait-independent finding is teosinte-presence recovery: the block/bin-aggregated callers (RTIGER 0.111; binhmm 0.101) recover the established BzeaSeq fraction (~0.10), while per-SNP calling (control) and the per-SNP HMM recalls (nNIL, LB-Impute) under-call. Genomic inflation is phenotype-dependent (reported per caller below). Caller genotypes are population-level and trait-independent; only the phenotype (StPi) changes.


Show code
callers <- c(control = "hwe_post_gt", nNIL = "nnil_mosaic", RTIGER = "rtiger_mosaic", `LB-Impute` = "lbimpute_mosaic", binhmm = "binhmm_mosaic")
gg <- fread(here("data/teonam/stpi_candidate_genes.tsv"))[, chr := as.integer(chr)]
min_cand_q <- function(sc) {
  q <- qval(sc$P)
  vals <- unlist(lapply(seq_len(nrow(gg)), function(i) {
    idx <- which(sc$CHR == gg$chr[i] & abs(sc$BP - gg$start[i]) <= 5e5)
    if (length(idx)) min(q[idx]) else NA_real_
  }))
  if (all(is.na(vals))) NA_real_ else signif(min(vals, na.rm = TRUE), 2)
}
ctab2 <- rbindlist(lapply(names(callers), function(nm) {
  g <- callers[[nm]]
  sc <- fread(here(sprintf("data/zeal/stpi_gwas_mlm_%s_donor_accession_snp50k.csv", g)))
  mf <- here(sprintf("data/zeal/zeal_%s.rds", g))
  pres <- if (file.exists(mf)) {
    st <- readRDS(mf)$state
    round(mean(st %in% c(1L, 2L), na.rm = TRUE), 3)
  } else {
    NA_real_
  }
  sc <- sc[is.finite(P) & P > 0]
  data.table(
    caller = nm, `teosinte presence` = pres, lambda_GC = lam(sc$P),
    `n FDR<=0.05` = sum(qval(sc$P) <= ALPHA),
    `best candidate q (BH)` = min_cand_q(sc)
  )
}))
knitr::kable(ctab2, caption = "StPi MLM (Taxon+K) by ancestry caller at 0.4×, with n markers at BH FDR ≤ 0.05 and the best (min) q across the anthocyanin candidate windows.")
